func(*, like=None):\\n456:             pass\\n457: \\n458:         func_with_like = array_function_dispatch()(func)\\n459:         with pytest.raises(TypeError):\\n460:             func_with_like()\\n461:         with pytest.raises(TypeError):\\n462:             func_with_like(like=234)\\n463: \\n464:     def test_too_many_args(self):\\n465:         # Mainly a unit-test to increase coverage\\n466:         objs = []\\n467:         for i in range(80):\\n468:             class MyArr:\\n469:                 def __array_function__(self, *args, **kwargs):\\n470:                     return NotImplemented\\n471: \\n472:             objs.append(MyArr())\\n473: \\n474:         def _dispatch(*args):\\n475:             return args\\n476: \\n477:         @array_function_dispatch(_dispatch)\\n478:         def func(*args):\\n479:             pass\\n480: \\n481:         with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\\\"maximum number\\\"):\\n482:             func(*objs)\\n483: \\n484: \\n485: \\n486: class TestNDArrayMethods:\\n487: \\n488:     def test_repr(self):\\n489:         # gh-12162: should still be defined even if __array_function__ doesn't\\n490:         # implement np.array_repr()\\n491: \\n492:         class MyArray(np.ndarray):\\n493:             def __array_function__(*args, **kwargs):\\n494:                 return NotImplemented\\n495: \\n496:         array = np.array(1).view(MyArray)\\n497:         assert_equal(repr(array), 'MyArray(1)')\\n498:         assert_equal(str(array), '1')\\n499: \\n500: \\n501: class TestNumPyFunctions:\\n502: \\n503:     def test_set_module(self):\\n504:         assert_equal(np.sum.__module__, 'numpy')\\n505:         assert_equal(np.char.equal.__module__, 'numpy.char')\\n506:         assert_equal(np.fft.fft.__module__, 'numpy.fft')\\n507:         assert_equal(np.linalg.solve.__module__, 'numpy.linalg')\\n508: \\n509:     def test_inspect_sum(self):\\n510:         signature = inspect.signature(np.sum)\\n511:         assert_('axis' in signature.parameters)\\n512: \\n513:     def test_override_sum(self):\\n514:         MyArray, implements = _new_duck_type_and_implements()\\n515: \\n516:         @implements(np.sum)\\n517:         def _(array):\\n518:             return 'yes'\\n519: \\n520:         assert_equal(np.sum(MyArray()), 'yes')\\n521: \\n522:     def test_sum_on_mock_array(self):\\n523: \\n524:         # We need a proxy for mocks because __array_function__ is only looked\\n525:         # up in the class dict\\n526:         class ArrayProxy:\\n527:             def __init__(self, value):\\n528:                 self.value = value\\n529:             def __array_function__(self, *args, **kwargs):\\n530:                 return self.value.__array_function__(*args, **kwargs)\\n531:             def __array__(self, *args, **kwargs):\\n532:                 return self.value.__array__(*args, **kwargs)\\n533: \\n534:         proxy = ArrayProxy(mock.Mock(spec=ArrayProxy))\\n535:         proxy.value.__array_function__.return_value = 1\\n536:         result = np.sum(proxy)\\n537:         assert_equal(result, 1)\\n538:         proxy.value.__array_function__.assert_called_once_with(\\n539:             np.sum, (ArrayProxy,), (proxy,), {})\\n540:         proxy.value.__array__.assert_not_called()\\n541: \\n542:     def test_sum_forwarding_implementation(self):\\n543: \\n544:         class MyArray(np.ndarray):\\n545: \\n546:             def sum(self, axis, out):\\n547:                 return 'summed'\\n548: \\n549:             def __array_function__(self, func, types, args, kwargs):\\n550:                 return super().__array_function__(func, types, args, kwargs)\\n551: \\n552:         # note: the internal implementation of np.sum() calls the .sum() method\\n553:         array = np.array(1).view(MyArray)\\n554:         assert_equal(np.sum(array), 'summed')\\n555: \\n556: \\n557: class TestArrayLike:\\n558:     def setup_method(self):\\n559:         class MyArray:\\n560:             def __init__(self, function=None):\\n561:                 self.function = function\\n562: \\n563:             def __array_function__(self, func, types, args, kwargs):\\n564:                 assert func is getattr(np, func.__name__)\\n565:                 try:\\n566:                     my_func = getattr(self, func.__name__)\\n567:                 except AttributeError:\\n568:                     return NotImplemented\\n569:                 return my_func(*args, **kwargs)\\n570: \\n571:         self.MyArray = MyArray\\n572: \\n573:         class MyNoArrayFunctionArray:\\n574:             def __init__(self, function=None):\\n575:                 self.function = function\\n576: \\n577:         self.MyNoArrayFunctionArray = MyNoArrayFunctionArray\\n578: \\n579:         class MySubclass(np.ndarray):\\n580:             def __array_function__(self, func, types, args, kwargs):\\n581:                 result = super().__array_function__(func, types, args, kwargs)\\n582:                 return result.view(self.__class__)\\n583: \\n584:         self.MySubclass = MySubclass\\n585: \\n586:     def add_method(self, name, arr_class, enable_value_error=False):\\n587:         def _definition(*args, **kwargs):\\n588:             # Check that `like=` isn't propagated downstream\\n589:             assert 'like' not in kwargs\\n590: \\n591:             if enable_value_error and 'value_error' in kwargs:\\n592:                 raise ValueError\\n593: \\n594:             return arr_class(getattr(arr_class, name))\\n595:         setattr(arr_class, name, _definition)\\n596: \\n597:     def func_args(*args, **kwargs):\\n598:         return args, kwargs\\n599: \\n600:     def test_array_like_not_implemented(self):\\n601:         self.add_method('array', self.MyArray)\\n602: \\n603:         ref = self.MyArray.array()\\n604: \\n605:         with assert_raises_regex(TypeError, 'no implementation found'):\\n606:             array_like = np.asarray(1, like=ref)\\n607: \\n608:     _array_tests = [\\n609:         ('array', *func_args((1,))),\\n610:         ('asarray', *func_args((1,))),\\n611:         ('asanyarray', *func_args((1,))),\\n612:         ('ascontiguousarray', *func_args((2, 3))),\\n613:         ('asfortranarray', *func_args((2, 3))),\\n614:         ('require', *func_args((np.arange(6).reshape(2, 3),),\\n615:                                requirements=['A', 'F'])),\\n616:         ('empty', *func_args((1,))),\\n617:         ('full', *func_args((1,), 2)),\\n618:         ('ones', *func_args((1,))),\\n619:         ('zeros', *func_args((1,))),\\n620:         ('arange', *func_args(3)),\\n621:         ('frombuffer', *func_args(b'\\\\x00' * 8, dtype=int)),\\n622:         ('fromiter', *func_args(range(3), dtype=int)),\\n623:         ('fromstring', *func_args('1,2', dtype=int, sep=',')),\\n624:         ('loadtxt', *func_args(lambda: StringIO('0 1\\\\n2 3'))),\\n625:         ('genfromtxt', *func_args(lambda: StringIO('1,2.1'),\\n626:                                   dtype=[('int', 'i8'), ('float', 'f8')],\\n627:                                   delimiter=',')),\\n628:     ]\\n629: \\n630: \\n631:     def test_nep35_functions_as_array_functions(self,):\\n632:         all_array_functions = get_overridable_numpy_array_functions()\\n633:         like_array_functions_subset = {\\n634:             getattr(np, func_name) for func_name, *_ in self.__class__._array_tests\\n635:         }\\n636:         assert like_array_functions_subset.issubset(all_array_functions)\\n637: \\n638:         nep35_python_functions = {\\n639:             np.eye, np.fromfunction, np.full, np.genfromtxt,\\n640:             np.identity, np.loadtxt, np.ones, np.require, np.tri,\\n641:         }\\n642:         assert nep35_python_functions.issubset(all_array_functions)\\n643: \\n644:         nep35_C_functions = {\\n645:             np.arange, np.array, np.asanyarray, np.asarray,\\n646:             np.ascontiguousarray, np.asfortranarray, np.empty,\\n647:             np.frombuffer, np.fromfile, np.fromiter, np.fromstring,\\n648:             np.zeros,\\n649:         }\\n650:         assert nep35_C_functions.issubset(all_array_functions)\\n651: \\n652:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('function, args, kwargs', _array_tests)\\n653:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('numpy_ref', [True, False])\\n654:     def test_array_like(self, function, args, kwargs, numpy_ref):\\n655:         self.add_method('array', self.MyArray)\\n656:         self.add_method(function, self.MyArray)\\n657:         np_func = getattr(np, function)\\n658:         my_func = getattr(self.MyArray, function)\\n659: \\n660:         if numpy_ref is True:\\n661:             ref = np.array(1)\\n662:         else:\\n663:             ref = self.MyArray.array()\\n664: \\n665:         like_args = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\\n666:         array_like = np_func(*like_args, **kwargs, like=ref)\\n667: \\n668:         if numpy_ref is True:\\n669:             assert type(array_like) is np.ndarray\\n670: \\n671:             np_args = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\\n672:             np_arr = np_func(*np_args, **kwargs)\\n673: \\n674:             # Special-case np.empty to ensure values match\\n675:             if function == \\\"empty\\\":\\n676:                 np_arr.fill(1)\\n677:                 array_like.fill(1)\\n678: \\n679:             assert_equal(array_like, np_arr)\\n680:         else:\\n681:             assert type(array_like) is self.MyArray\\n682:             assert array_like.function is my_func\\n683: \\n684:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('function, args, kwargs', _array_tests)\\n685:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('ref', [1, [1], \\\"MyNoArrayFunctionArray\\\"])\\n686:     def test_no_array_function_like(self, function, args, kwargs, ref):\\n687:         self.add_method('array', self.MyNoArrayFunctionArray)\\n688:         self.add_method(function, self.MyNoArrayFunctionArray)\\n689:         np_func = getattr(np, function)\\n690: \\n691:         # Instantiate ref if it's the MyNoArrayFunctionArray class\\n692:         if ref == \\\"MyNoArrayFunctionArray\\\":\\n693:             ref = self.MyNoArrayFunctionArray.array()\\n694: \\n695:         like_args = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\\n696: \\n697:         with assert_raises_regex(TypeError,\\n698:                 'The `like` argument must be an array-like that implements'):\\n699:             np_func(*like_args, **kwargs, like=ref)\\n700: \\n701:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('function, args, kwargs', _array_tests)\\n702:     def test_subclass(self, function, args, kwargs):\\n703:         ref = np.array(1).view(self.MySubclass)\\n704:         np_func = getattr(np, function)\\n705:         like_args = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\\n706:         array_like = np_func(*like_args, **kwargs, like=ref)\\n707:         assert type(array_like) is self.MySubclass\\n708:         if np_func is np.empty:\\n709:             return\\n710:         np_args = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\\n711:         np_arr = np_func(*np_args, **kwargs)\\n712:         assert_equal(array_like.view(np.ndarray), np_arr)\\n713: \\n714:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('numpy_ref', [True, False])\\n715:     def test_array_like_fromfile(self, numpy_ref):\\n716:         self.add_method('array', self.MyArray)\\n717:         self.add_method(\\\"fromfile\\\", self.MyArray)\\n718: \\n719:         if numpy_ref is True:\\n720:             ref = np.array(1)\\n721:         else:\\n722:             ref = self.MyArray.array()\\n723: \\n724:         data = np.random.random(5)\\n725: \\n726:         with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\\n727:             fname = os.path.join(tmpdir, \\\"testfile\\\")\\n728:             data.tofile(fname)\\n729: \\n730:             array_like = np.fromfile(fname, like=ref)\\n731:             if numpy_ref is True:\\n732:                 assert type(array_like) is np.ndarray\\n733:                 np_res = np.fromfile(fname, like=ref)\\n734:                 assert_equal(np_res, data)\\n735:                 assert_equal(array_like, np_res)\\n736:             else:\\n737:                 assert type(array_like) is self.MyArray\\n738:                 assert array_like.function is self.MyArray.fromfile\\n739: \\n740:     def test_exception_handling(self):\\n741:         self.add_method('array', self.MyArray, enable_value_error=True)\\n742: \\n743:         ref = self.MyArray.array()\\n744: \\n745:         with assert_raises(TypeError):\\n746:             # Raises the error about `value_error` being invalid first\\n747:             np.array(1, value_error=True, like=ref)\\n748: \\n749:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('function, args, kwargs', _array_tests)\\n750:     def test_like_as_none(self, function, args, kwargs):\\n751:         self.add_method('array', self.MyArray)\\n752:         self.add_method(function, self.MyArray)\\n753:         np_func = getattr(np, function)\\n754: \\n755:         like_args = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\\n756:         # required for loadtxt and genfromtxt to init w/o error.\\n757:         like_args_exp = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\\n758: \\n759:         array_like = np_func(*like_args, **kwargs, like=None)\\n760:         expected = np_func(*like_args_exp, **kwargs)\\n761:         # Special-case np.empty to ensure values match\\n762:         if function == \\\"empty\\\":\\n763:             array_like.fill(1)\\n764:             expected.fill(1)\\n765:         assert_equal(array_like, expected)\\n766: \\n767: \\n768: def test_function_like():\\n769:     # We provide a `__get__` implementation, make sure it works\\n770:     assert type(np.mean) is np._core._multiarray_umath._ArrayFunctionDispatcher\\n771: \\n772:     class MyClass:\\n773:         def __array__(self, dtype=None, copy=None):\\n774:             # valid argument to mean:\\n775:             return np.arange(3)\\n776: \\n777:         func1 = staticmethod(np.mean)\\n778:         func2 = np.mean\\n779:         func3 = classmethod(np.mean)\\n780: \\n781:     m = MyClass()\\n782:     assert m.func1([10]) == 10\\n783:     assert m.func2() == 1  # mean of the arange\\n784:     with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\\\"unsupported operand type\\\"):\\n785:         # Tries to operate on the class\\n786:         m.func3()\\n787: \\n788:     # Manual binding also works (the above may shortcut):\\n789:     bound = np.mean.__get__(m, MyClass)\\n790:     assert bound() == 1\\n791: \\n792:     bound = np.mean.__get__(None, MyClass)  # unbound actually\\n793:     assert bound([10]) == 10\\n794: \\n795:     bound = np.mean.__get__(MyClass)  # classmethod\\n796:     with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\\\"unsupported operand type\\\"):\\n797:         bound()\\n798: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2@9ca88bff35bf60de74f9f95e6f90e6b4e0913e78/-/blob/numpy/_core/tests/test_overrides.py\"}",

    "content": "1: import inspect\n2: import sys\n3: import os\n4: import tempfile\n5: from io import StringIO\n6: from unittest import mock\n7: import pickle\n8: \n9: import pytest\n10: \n11: import numpy as np\n12: from numpy.testing import (\n13:     assert_, assert_equal, assert_raises, assert_raises_regex)\n14: from numpy.testing.overrides import get_overridable_numpy_array_functions\n15: from numpy._core.overrides import (\n16:     _get_implementing_args, array_function_dispatch,\n17:     verify_matching_signatures)\n18: \n19: def _return_not_implemented(self, *args, **kwargs):\n20:     return NotImplemented\n21: \n22: \n23: # need to define this at the top level to test pickling\n24: @array_function_dispatch(lambda array: (array,))\n25: def dispatched_one_arg(array):\n26:     \"\"\"Docstring.\"\"\"\n27:     return 'original'\n28: \n29: \n30: @array_function_dispatch(lambda array1, array2: (array1, array2))\n31: def dispatched_two_arg(array1, array2):\n32:     \"\"\"Docstring.\"\"\"\n33:     return 'original'\n34: \n35: \n36: class TestGetImplementingArgs:\n37: \n38:     def test_ndarray(self):\n39:         array = np.array(1)\n40: \n41:         args = _get_implementing_args([array])\n42:         assert_equal(list(args), [array])\n43: \n44:         args = _get_implementing_args([array, array])\n45:         assert_equal(list(args), [array])\n46: \n47:         args = _get_implementing_args([array, 1])\n48:         assert_equal(list(args), [array])\n49: \n50:         args = _get_implementing_args([1, array])\n51:         assert_equal(list(args), [array])\n52: \n53:     def test_ndarray_subclasses(self):\n54: \n55:         class OverrideSub(np.ndarray):\n56:             __array_function__ = _return_not_implemented\n57: \n58:         class NoOverrideSub(np.ndarray):\n59:             pass\n60: \n61:         array = np.array(1).view(np.ndarray)\n62:         override_sub = np.array(1).view(OverrideSub)\n63:         no_override_sub = np.array(1).view(NoOverrideSub)\n64: \n65:         args = _get_implementing_args([array, override_sub])\n66:         assert_equal(list(args), [override_sub, array])\n67: \n68:         args = _get_implementing_args([array, no_override_sub])\n69:         assert_equal(list(args), [no_override_sub, array])\n70: \n71:         args = _get_implementing_args(\n72:             [override_sub, no_override_sub])\n73:         assert_equal(list(args), [override_sub, no_override_sub])\n74: \n75:     def test_ndarray_and_duck_array(self):\n76: \n77:         class Other:\n78:             __array_function__ = _return_not_implemented\n79: \n80:         array = np.array(1)\n81:         other = Other()\n82: \n83:         args = _get_implementing_args([other, array])\n84:         assert_equal(list(args), [other, array])\n85: \n86:         args = _get_implementing_args([array, other])\n87:         assert_equal(list(args), [array, other])\n88: \n89:     def test_ndarray_subclass_and_duck_array(self):\n90: \n91:         class OverrideSub(np.ndarray):\n92:             __array_function__ = _return_not_implemented\n93: \n94:         class Other:\n95:             __array_function__ = _return_not_implemented\n96: \n97:         array = np.array(1)\n98:         subarray = np.array(1).view(OverrideSub)\n99:         other = Other()\n100: \n101:         assert_equal(_get_implementing_args([array, subarray, other]),\n102:                      [subarray, array, other])\n103:         assert_equal(_get_implementing_args([array, other, subarray]),\n104:                      [subarray, array, other])\n105: \n106:     def test_many_duck_arrays(self):\n107: \n108:         class A:\n109:             __array_function__ = _return_not_implemented\n110: \n111:         class B(A):\n112:             __array_function__ = _return_not_implemented\n113: \n114:         class C(A):\n115:             __array_function__ = _return_not_implemented\n116: \n117:         class D:\n118:             __array_function__ = _return_not_implemented\n119: \n120:         a = A()\n121:         b = B()\n122:         c = C()\n123:         d = D()\n124: \n125:         assert_equal(_get_implementing_args([1]), [])\n126:         assert_equal(_get_implementing_args([a]), [a])\n127:         assert_equal(_get_implementing_args([a, 1]), [a])\n128:         assert_equal(_get_implementing_args([a, a, a]), [a])\n129:         assert_equal(_get_implementing_args([a, d, a]), [a, d])\n130:         assert_equal(_get_implementing_args([a, b]), [b, a])\n131:         assert_equal(_get_implementing_args([b, a]), [b, a])\n132:         assert_equal(_get_implementing_args([a, b, c]), [b, c, a])\n133:         assert_equal(_get_implementing_args([a, c, b]), [c, b, a])\n134: \n135:     def test_too_many_duck_arrays(self):\n136:         namespace = dict(__array_function__=_return_not_implemented)\n137:         types = [type('A' + str(i), (object,), namespace) for i in range(65)]\n138:         relevant_args = [t() for t in types]\n139: \n140:         actual = _get_implementing_args(relevant_args[:64])\n141:         assert_equal(actual, relevant_args[:64])\n142: \n143:         with assert_raises_regex(TypeError, 'distinct argument types'):\n144:             _get_implementing_args(relevant_args)\n145: \n146: \n147: class TestNDArrayArrayFunction:\n148: \n149:     def test_method(self):\n150: \n151:         class Other:\n152:             __array_function__ = _return_not_implemented\n153: \n154:         class NoOverrideSub(np.ndarray):\n155:             pass\n156: \n157:         class OverrideSub(np.ndarray):\n158:             __array_function__ = _return_not_implemented\n159: \n160:         array = np.array([1])\n161:         other = Other()\n162:         no_override_sub = array.view(NoOverrideSub)\n163:         override_sub = array.view(OverrideSub)\n164: \n165:         result = array.__array_function__(func=dispatched_two_arg,\n166:                                           types=(np.ndarray,),\n167:                                           args=(array, 1.), kwargs={})\n168:         assert_equal(result, 'original')\n169: \n170:         result = array.__array_function__(func=dispatched_two_arg,\n171:                                           types=(np.ndarray, Other),\n172:                                           args=(array, other), kwargs={})\n173:         assert_(result is NotImplemented)\n174: \n175:         result = array.__array_function__(func=dispatched_two_arg,\n176:                                           types=(np.ndarray, NoOverrideSub),\n177:                                           args=(array, no_override_sub),\n178:                                           kwargs={})\n179:         assert_equal(result, 'original')\n180: \n181:         result = array.__array_function__(func=dispatched_two_arg,\n182:                                           types=(np.ndarray, OverrideSub),\n183:                                           args=(array, override_sub),\n184:                                           kwargs={})\n185:         assert_equal(result, 'original')\n186: \n187:         with assert_raises_regex(TypeError, 'no implementation found'):\n188:             np.concatenate((array, other))\n189: \n190:         expected = np.concatenate((array, array))\n191:         result = np.concatenate((array, no_override_sub))\n192:         assert_equal(result, expected.view(NoOverrideSub))\n193:         result = np.concatenate((array, override_sub))\n194:         assert_equal(result, expected.view(OverrideSub))\n195: \n196:     def test_no_wrapper(self):\n197:         # Regular numpy functions have wrappers, but do not presume\n198:         # all functions do (array creation ones do not): check that\n199:         # we just call the function in that case.\n200:         array = np.array(1)\n201:         func = lambda x: x * 2\n202:         result = array.__array_function__(func=func, types=(np.ndarray,),\n203:                                           args=(array,), kwargs={})\n204:         assert_equal(result, array * 2)\n205: \n206:     def test_wrong_arguments(self):\n207:         # Check our implementation guards against wrong arguments.\n208:         a = np.array([1, 2])\n209:         with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\"args must be a tuple\"):\n210:             a.__array_function__(np.reshape, (np.ndarray,), a, (2, 1))\n211:         with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\"kwargs must be a dict\"):\n212:             a.__array_function__(np.reshape, (np.ndarray,), (a,), (2, 1))\n213: \n214:     def test_wrong_arguments(self):\n215:         # Check our implementation guards against wrong arguments.\n216:         a = np.array([1, 2])\n217:         with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\"args must be a tuple\"):\n218:             a.__array_function__(np.reshape, (np.ndarray,), a, (2, 1))\n219:         with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\"kwargs must be a dict\"):\n220:             a.__array_function__(np.reshape, (np.ndarray,), (a,), (2, 1))\n221: \n222: \n223: class TestArrayFunctionDispatch:\n224: \n225:     def test_pickle(self):\n226:         for proto in range(2, pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL + 1):\n227:             roundtripped = pickle.loads(\n228:                     pickle.dumps(dispatched_one_arg, protocol=proto))\n229:             assert_(roundtripped is dispatched_one_arg)\n230: \n231:     def test_name_and_docstring(self):\n232:         assert_equal(dispatched_one_arg.__name__, 'dispatched_one_arg')\n233:         if sys.flags.optimize < 2:\n234:             assert_equal(dispatched_one_arg.__doc__, 'Docstring.')\n235: \n236:     def test_interface(self):\n237: \n238:         class MyArray:\n239:             def __array_function__(self, func, types, args, kwargs):\n240:                 return (self, func, types, args, kwargs)\n241: \n242:         original = MyArray()\n243:         (obj, func, types, args, kwargs) = dispatched_one_arg(original)\n244:         assert_(obj is original)\n245:         assert_(func is dispatched_one_arg)\n246:         assert_equal(set(types), {MyArray})\n247:         # assert_equal uses the overloaded np.iscomplexobj() internally\n248:         assert_(args == (original,))\n249:         assert_equal(kwargs, {})\n250: \n251:     def test_not_implemented(self):\n252: \n253:         class MyArray:\n254:             def __array_function__(self, func, types, args, kwargs):\n255:                 return NotImplemented\n256: \n257:         array = MyArray()\n258:         with assert_raises_regex(TypeError, 'no implementation found'):\n259:             dispatched_one_arg(array)\n260: \n261:     def test_where_dispatch(self):\n262: \n263:         class DuckArray:\n264:             def __array_function__(self, ufunc, method, *inputs, **kwargs):\n265:                 return \"overridden\"\n266: \n267:         array = np.array(1)\n268:         duck_array = DuckArray()\n269: \n270:         result = np.std(array, where=duck_array)\n271: \n272:         assert_equal(result, \"overridden\")\n273: \n274: \n275: class TestVerifyMatchingSignatures:\n276: \n277:     def test_verify_matching_signatures(self):\n278: \n279:         verify_matching_signatures(lambda x: 0, lambda x: 0)\n280:         verify_matching_signatures(lambda x=None: 0, lambda x=None: 0)\n281:         verify_matching_signatures(lambda x=1: 0, lambda x=None: 0)\n282: \n283:         with assert_raises(RuntimeError):\n284:             verify_matching_signatures(lambda a: 0, lambda b: 0)\n285:         with assert_raises(RuntimeError):\n286:             verify_matching_signatures(lambda x: 0, lambda x=None: 0)\n287:         with assert_raises(RuntimeError):\n288:             verify_matching_signatures(lambda x=None: 0, lambda y=None: 0)\n289:         with assert_raises(RuntimeError):\n290:             verify_matching_signatures(lambda x=1: 0, lambda y=1: 0)\n291: \n292:     def test_array_function_dispatch(self):\n293: \n294:         with assert_raises(RuntimeError):\n295:             @array_function_dispatch(lambda x: (x,))\n296:             def f(y):\n297:                 pass\n298: \n299:         # should not raise\n300:         @array_function_dispatch(lambda x: (x,), verify=False)\n301:         def f(y):\n302:             pass\n303: \n304: \n305: def _new_duck_type_and_implements():\n306:     \"\"\"Create a duck array type and implements functions.\"\"\"\n307:     HANDLED_FUNCTIONS = {}\n308: \n309:     class MyArray:\n310:         def __array_function__(self, func, types, args, kwargs):\n311:             if func not in HANDLED_FUNCTIONS:\n312:                 return NotImplemented\n313:             if not all(issubclass(t, MyArray) for t in types):\n314:                 return NotImplemented\n315:             return HANDLED_FUNCTIONS[func](*args, **kwargs)\n316: \n317:     def implements(numpy_function):\n318:         \"\"\"Register an __array_function__ implementations.\"\"\"\n319:         def decorator(func):\n320:             HANDLED_FUNCTIONS[numpy_function] = func\n321:             return func\n322:         return decorator\n323: \n324:     return (MyArray, implements)\n325: \n326: \n327: class TestArrayFunctionImplementation:\n328: \n329:     def test_one_arg(self):\n330:         MyArray, implements = _new_duck_type_and_implements()\n331: \n332:         @implements(dispatched_one_arg)\n333:         def _(array):\n334:             return 'myarray'\n335: \n336:         assert_equal(dispatched_one_arg(1), 'original')\n337:         assert_equal(dispatched_one_arg(MyArray()), 'myarray')\n338: \n339:     def test_optional_args(self):\n340:         MyArray, implements = _new_duck_type_and_implements()\n341: \n342:         @array_function_dispatch(lambda array, option=None: (array,))\n343:         def func_with_option(array, option='default'):\n344:             return option\n345: \n346:         @implements(func_with_option)\n347:         def my_array_func_with_option(array, new_option='myarray'):\n348:             return new_option\n349: \n350:         # we don't need to implement every option on __array_function__\n351:         # implementations\n352:         assert_equal(func_with_option(1), 'default')\n353:         assert_equal(func_with_option(1, option='extra'), 'extra')\n354:         assert_equal(func_with_option(MyArray()), 'myarray')\n355:         with assert_raises(TypeError):\n356:             func_with_option(MyArray(), option='extra')\n357: \n358:         # but new options on implementations can't be used\n359:         result = my_array_func_with_option(MyArray(), new_option='yes')\n360:         assert_equal(result, 'yes')\n361:         with assert_raises(TypeError):\n362:             func_with_option(MyArray(), new_option='no')\n363: \n364:     def test_not_implemented(self):\n365:         MyArray, implements = _new_duck_type_and_implements()\n366: \n367:         @array_function_dispatch(lambda array: (array,), module='my')\n368:         def func(array):\n369:             return array\n370: \n371:         array = np.array(1)\n372:         assert_(func(array) is array)\n373:         assert_equal(func.__module__, 'my')\n374: \n375:         with assert_raises_regex(\n376:                 TypeError, \"no implementation found for 'my.func'\"):\n377:             func(MyArray())\n378: \n379:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"name\", [\"concatenate\", \"mean\", \"asarray\"])\n380:     def test_signature_error_message_simple(self, name):\n381:         func = getattr(np, name)\n382:         try:\n383:             # all of these functions need an argument:\n384:             func()\n385:         except TypeError as e:\n386:             exc = e\n387: \n388:         assert exc.args[0].startswith(f\"{name}()\")\n389: \n390:     def test_signature_error_message(self):\n391:         # The lambda function will be named \"<lambda>\", but the TypeError\n392:         # should show the name as \"func\"\n393:         def _dispatcher():\n394:             return ()\n395: \n396:         @array_function_dispatch(_dispatcher)\n397:         def func():\n398:             pass\n399: \n400:         try:\n401:             func._implementation(bad_arg=3)\n402:         except TypeError as e:\n403:             expected_exception = e\n404: \n405:         try:\n406:             func(bad_arg=3)\n407:             raise AssertionError(\"must fail\")\n408:         except TypeError as exc:\n409:             if exc.args[0].startswith(\"_dispatcher\"):\n410:                 # We replace the qualname currently, but it used `__name__`\n411:                 # (relevant functions have the same name and qualname anyway)\n412:                 pytest.skip(\"Python version is not using __qualname__ for \"\n413:                             \"TypeError formatting.\")\n414: \n415:             assert exc.args == expected_exception.args\n416: \n417:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"value\", [234, \"this func is not replaced\"])\n418:     def test_dispatcher_error(self, value):\n419:         # If the dispatcher raises an error, we must not attempt to mutate it\n420:         error = TypeError(value)\n421: \n422:         def dispatcher():\n423:             raise error\n424: \n425:         @array_function_dispatch(dispatcher)\n426:         def func():\n427:             return 3\n428: \n429:         try:\n430:             func()\n431:             raise AssertionError(\"must fail\")\n432:         except TypeError as exc:\n433:             assert exc is error  # unmodified exception\n434: \n435:     def test_properties(self):\n436:         # Check that str and repr are sensible\n437:         func = dispatched_two_arg\n438:         assert str(func) == str(func._implementation)\n439:         repr_no_id = repr(func).split(\"at \")[0]\n440:         repr_no_id_impl = repr(func._implementation).split(\"at \")[0]\n441:         assert repr_no_id == repr_no_id_impl\n442: \n443:     @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"func\", [\n444:             lambda x, y: 0,  # no like argument\n445:             lambda like=None: 0,  # not keyword only\n446:             lambda *, like=None, a=3: 0,  # not last (not that it matters)\n447:         ])\n448:     def test_bad_like_sig(self, func):\n449:         # We sanity check the signature, and these should fail.\n450:         with pytest.raises(RuntimeError):\n451:             array_function_dispatch()(func)\n452: \n453:     def test_bad_like_passing(self):\n454:         # Cover internal sanity check for passing like as first positional arg\n455:         def func(*, like=None):\n456:             pass\n457: \n458:         func_with_like = array_function_dispatch()(func)\n459:         with pytest.raises(TypeError):\n460:             func_with_like()\n461:         with pytest.raises(TypeError):\n462:             func_with_like(like=234)\n463: \n464:     def test_too_many_args(self):\n465:         # Mainly a unit-test to increase coverage\n466:         objs = []\n467:         for i in range(80):\n468:             class MyArr:\n469:                 def __array_function__(self, *args, **kwargs):\n470:                     return NotImplemented\n471: \n472:             objs.append(MyArr())\n473: \n474:         def _dispatch(*args):\n475:             return args\n476: \n477:         @array_function_dispatch(_dispatch)\n478:         def func(*args):\n479:             pass\n480: \n481:         with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\"maximum number\"):\n482:             func(*objs)\n483: \n484: \n485: \n486: class TestNDArrayMethods:\n487: \n488:     def test_repr(self):\n489:         # gh-12162: should still be defined even if __array_function__ doesn't\n490:         # implement np.array_repr()\n491: \n492:         class MyArray(np.ndarray):\n493:             def __array_function__(*args, **kwargs):\n494:                 return NotImplemented\n495: \n496:         array = np.array(1).view(MyArray)\n497:         assert_equal(repr(array), 'MyArray(1)')\n498:         assert_equal(str(array), '1')\n499: \n500: \n501: class TestNumPyFunctions:\n502: \n503:     def test_set_module(self):\n504:         assert_equal(np.sum.__module__, 'numpy')\n505:         assert_equal(np.char.equal.__module__, 'numpy.char')\n506:         assert_equal(np.fft.fft.__module__, 'numpy.fft')\n507:         assert_equal(np.linalg.solve.__module__, 'numpy.linalg')\n508: \n509:     def test_inspect_sum(self):\n510:         signature = inspect.signature(np.sum)\n511:         assert_('axis' in signature.parameters)\n512: \n513:     def test_override_sum(self):\n514:         MyArray, implements = _new_duck_type_and_implements()\n515: \n516:         @implements(np.sum)\n517:         def _(array):\n518:             return 'yes'\n519: \n520:         assert_equal(np.sum(MyArray()), 'yes')\n521: \n522:     def test_sum_on_mock_array(self):\n523: \n524:         # We need a proxy for mocks because __array_function__ is only looked\n525:         # up in the class dict\n526:         class ArrayProxy:\n527:             def __init__(self, value):\n528:                 self.value = value\n529:             def __array_function__(self, *args, **kwargs):\n530:                 return self.value.__array_function__(*args, **kwargs)\n531:             def __array__(self, *args, **kwargs):\n532:                 return self.value.__array__(*args, **kwargs)\n533: \n534:         proxy = ArrayProxy(mock.Mock(spec=ArrayProxy))\n535:         proxy.value.__array_function__.return_value = 1\n536:         result = np.sum(proxy)\n537:         assert_equal(result, 1)\n538:         proxy.value.__array_function__.assert_called_once_with(\n539:             np.sum, (ArrayProxy,), (proxy,), {})\n540:         proxy.value.__array__.assert_not_called()\n541: \n542:     def test_sum_forwarding_implementation(self):\n543: \n544:         class MyArray(np.ndarray):\n545: \n546:             def sum(self, axis, out):\n547:                 return 'summed'\n548: \n549:             def __array_function__(self, func, types, args, kwargs):\n550:                 return super().__array_function__(func, types, args, kwargs)\n551: \n552:         # note: the internal implementation of np.sum() calls the .sum() method\n553:         array = np.array(1).view(MyArray)\n554:         assert_equal(np.sum(array), 'summed')\n555: \n556: \n557: class TestArrayLike:\n558:     def setup_method(self):\n559:         class MyArray:\n560:             def __init__(self, function=None):\n561:                 self.function = function\n562: \n563:             def __array_function__(self, func, types, args, kwargs):\n564:                 assert func is getattr(np, func.__name__)\n565:                 try:\n566:                     my_func = getattr(self, func.__name__)\n567:                 except AttributeError:\n568:                     return NotImplemented\n569:                 return my_func(*args, **kwargs)\n570: \n571:         self.MyArray = MyArray\n572: \n573:         class MyNoArrayFunctionArray:\n574:             def __init__(self, function=None):\n575:                 self.function = function\n576: \n577:         self.MyNoArrayFunctionArray = MyNoArrayFunctionArray\n578: \n579:         class MySubclass(np.ndarray):\n580:             def __array_function__(self, func, types, args, kwargs):\n581:                 result = super().__array_function__(func, types, args, kwargs)\n582:                 return result.view(self.__class__)\n583: \n584:         self.MySubclass = MySubclass\n585: \n586:     def add_method(self, name, arr_class, enable_value_error=False):\n587:         def _definition(*args, **kwargs):\n588:             # Check that `like=` isn't propagated downstream\n589:             assert 'like' not in kwargs\n590: \n591:             if enable_value_error and 'value_error' in kwargs:\n592:                 raise ValueError\n593: \n594:             return arr_class(getattr(arr_class, name))\n595:         setattr(arr_class, name, _definition)\n596: \n597:     def func_args(*args, **kwargs):\n598:         return args, kwargs\n599: \n600:     def test_array_like_not_implemented(self):\n601:         self.add_method('array', self.MyArray)\n602: \n603:         ref = self.MyArray.array()\n604: \n605:         with assert_raises_regex(TypeError, 'no implementation found'):\n606:             array_like = np.asarray(1, like=ref)\n607: \n608:     _array_tests = [\n609:         ('array', *func_args((1,))),\n610:         ('asarray', *func_args((1,))),\n611:         ('asanyarray', *func_args((1,))),\n612:         ('ascontiguousarray', *func_args((2, 3))),\n613:         ('asfortranarray', *func_args((2, 3))),\n614:         ('require', *func_args((np.arange(6).reshape(2, 3),),\n615:                                requirements=['A', 'F'])),\n616:         ('empty', *func_args((1,))),\n617:         ('full', *func_args((1,), 2)),\n618:         ('ones', *func_args((1,))),\n619:         ('zeros', *func_args((1,))),\n620:         ('arange', *func_args(3)),\n621:         ('frombuffer', *func_args(b'\\x00' * 8, dtype=int)),\n622:         ('fromiter', *func_args(range(3), dtype=int)),\n623:         ('fromstring', *func_args('1,2', dtype=int, sep=',')),\n624:         ('loadtxt', *func_args(lambda: StringIO('0 1\\n2 3'))),\n625:         ('genfromtxt', *func_args(lambda: StringIO('1,2.1'),\n626:                                   dtype=[('int', 'i8'), ('float', 'f8')],\n627:                                   delimiter=',')),\n628:     ]\n629: \n630: \n631:     def test_nep35_functions_as_array_functions(self,):\n632:         all_array_functions = get_overridable_numpy_array_functions()\n633:         like_array_functions_subset = {\n634:             getattr(np, func_name) for func_name, *_ in self.__class__._array_tests\n635:         }\n636:         assert like_array_functions_subset.issubset(all_array_functions)\n637: \n638:         nep35_python_functions = {\n639:             np.eye, np.fromfunction, np.full, np.genfromtxt,\n640:             np.identity, np.loadtxt, np.ones, np.require, np.tri,\n641:         }\n642:         assert nep35_python_functions.issubset(all_array_functions)\n643: \n644:         nep35_C_functions = {\n645:             np.arange, np.array, np.asanyarray, np.asarray,\n646:             np.ascontiguousarray, np.asfortranarray, np.empty,\n647:             np.frombuffer, np.fromfile, np.fromiter, np.fromstring,\n648:             np.zeros,\n649:         }\n650:         assert nep35_C_functions.issubset(all_array_functions)\n651: \n652:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('function, args, kwargs', _array_tests)\n653:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('numpy_ref', [True, False])\n654:     def test_array_like(self, function, args, kwargs, numpy_ref):\n655:         self.add_method('array', self.MyArray)\n656:         self.add_method(function, self.MyArray)\n657:         np_func = getattr(np, function)\n658:         my_func = getattr(self.MyArray, function)\n659: \n660:         if numpy_ref is True:\n661:             ref = np.array(1)\n662:         else:\n663:             ref = self.MyArray.array()\n664: \n665:         like_args = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\n666:         array_like = np_func(*like_args, **kwargs, like=ref)\n667: \n668:         if numpy_ref is True:\n669:             assert type(array_like) is np.ndarray\n670: \n671:             np_args = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\n672:             np_arr = np_func(*np_args, **kwargs)\n673: \n674:             # Special-case np.empty to ensure values match\n675:             if function == \"empty\":\n676:                 np_arr.fill(1)\n677:                 array_like.fill(1)\n678: \n679:             assert_equal(array_like, np_arr)\n680:         else:\n681:             assert type(array_like) is self.MyArray\n682:             assert array_like.function is my_func\n683: \n684:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('function, args, kwargs', _array_tests)\n685:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('ref', [1, [1], \"MyNoArrayFunctionArray\"])\n686:     def test_no_array_function_like(self, function, args, kwargs, ref):\n687:         self.add_method('array', self.MyNoArrayFunctionArray)\n688:         self.add_method(function, self.MyNoArrayFunctionArray)\n689:         np_func = getattr(np, function)\n690: \n691:         # Instantiate ref if it's the MyNoArrayFunctionArray class\n692:         if ref == \"MyNoArrayFunctionArray\":\n693:             ref = self.MyNoArrayFunctionArray.array()\n694: \n695:         like_args = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\n696: \n697:         with assert_raises_regex(TypeError,\n698:                 'The `like` argument must be an array-like that implements'):\n699:             np_func(*like_args, **kwargs, like=ref)\n700: \n701:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('function, args, kwargs', _array_tests)\n702:     def test_subclass(self, function, args, kwargs):\n703:         ref = np.array(1).view(self.MySubclass)\n704:         np_func = getattr(np, function)\n705:         like_args = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\n706:         array_like = np_func(*like_args, **kwargs, like=ref)\n707:         assert type(array_like) is self.MySubclass\n708:         if np_func is np.empty:\n709:             return\n710:         np_args = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\n711:         np_arr = np_func(*np_args, **kwargs)\n712:         assert_equal(array_like.view(np.ndarray), np_arr)\n713: \n714:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('numpy_ref', [True, False])\n715:     def test_array_like_fromfile(self, numpy_ref):\n716:         self.add_method('array', self.MyArray)\n717:         self.add_method(\"fromfile\", self.MyArray)\n718: \n719:         if numpy_ref is True:\n720:             ref = np.array(1)\n721:         else:\n722:             ref = self.MyArray.array()\n723: \n724:         data = np.random.random(5)\n725: \n726:         with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n727:             fname = os.path.join(tmpdir, \"testfile\")\n728:             data.tofile(fname)\n729: \n730:             array_like = np.fromfile(fname, like=ref)\n731:             if numpy_ref is True:\n732:                 assert type(array_like) is np.ndarray\n733:                 np_res = np.fromfile(fname, like=ref)\n734:                 assert_equal(np_res, data)\n735:                 assert_equal(array_like, np_res)\n736:             else:\n737:                 assert type(array_like) is self.MyArray\n738:                 assert array_like.function is self.MyArray.fromfile\n739: \n740:     def test_exception_handling(self):\n741:         self.add_method('array', self.MyArray, enable_value_error=True)\n742: \n743:         ref = self.MyArray.array()\n744: \n745:         with assert_raises(TypeError):\n746:             # Raises the error about `value_error` being invalid first\n747:             np.array(1, value_error=True, like=ref)\n748: \n749:     @pytest.mark.parametrize('function, args, kwargs', _array_tests)\n750:     def test_like_as_none(self, function, args, kwargs):\n751:         self.add_method('array', self.MyArray)\n752:         self.add_method(function, self.MyArray)\n753:         np_func = getattr(np, function)\n754: \n755:         like_args = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\n756:         # required for loadtxt and genfromtxt to init w/o error.\n757:         like_args_exp = tuple(a() if callable(a) else a for a in args)\n758: \n759:         array_like = np_func(*like_args, **kwargs, like=None)\n760:         expected = np_func(*like_args_exp, **kwargs)\n761:         # Special-case np.empty to ensure values match\n762:         if function == \"empty\":\n763:             array_like.fill(1)\n764:             expected.fill(1)\n765:         assert_equal(array_like, expected)\n766: \n767: \n768: def test_function_like():\n769:     # We provide a `__get__` implementation, make sure it works\n770:     assert type(np.mean) is np._core._multiarray_umath._ArrayFunctionDispatcher\n771: \n772:     class MyClass:\n773:         def __array__(self, dtype=None, copy=None):\n774:             # valid argument to mean:\n775:             return np.arange(3)\n776: \n777:         func1 = staticmethod(np.mean)\n778:         func2 = np.mean\n779:         func3 = classmethod(np.mean)\n780: \n781:     m = MyClass()\n782:     assert m.func1([10]) == 10\n783:     assert m.func2() == 1  # mean of the arange\n784:     with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\"unsupported operand type\"):\n785:         # Tries to operate on the class\n786:         m.func3()\n787: \n788:     # Manual binding also works (the above may shortcut):\n789:     bound = np.mean.__get__(m, MyClass)\n790:     assert bound() == 1\n791: \n792:     bound = np.mean.__get__(None, MyClass)  # unbound actually\n793:     assert bound([10]) == 10\n794: \n795:     bound = np.mean.__get__(MyClass)  # classmethod\n796:     with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=\"unsupported operand type\"):\n797:         bound()\n798: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"Implementation of __array_function__ overrides from NEP-18.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: import collections\\n3: import functools\\n4: \\n5: from .._utils import set_module\\n6: from .._utils._inspect import getargspec\\n7: from numpy._core._multiarray_umath import (\\n8:     add_docstring,  _get_implementing_args, _ArrayFunctionDispatcher)\\n9: \\n10: \\n11: ARRAY_FUNCTIONS = set()\\n12: \\n13: array_function_like_doc = (\\n14:     \\\"\\\"\\\"like : array_like, optional\\n15:         Reference object to allow the creation of arrays which are not\\n16:         NumPy arrays. If an array-like passed in as ``like`` supports\\n17:         the ``__array_function__`` protocol, the result will be defined\\n18:         by it. In this case, it ensures the creation of an array object\\n19:         compatible with that passed in via this argument.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n20: )\\n21: \\n22: def get_array_function_like_doc(public_api, docstring_template=\\\"\\\"):\\n23:     ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\\n24:     docstring = public_api.__doc__ or docstring_template\\n25:     return docstring.replace(\\\"${ARRAY_FUNCTION_LIKE}\\\", array_function_like_doc)\\n26: \\n27: def finalize_array_function_like(public_api):\\n28:     public_api.__doc__ = get_array_function_like_doc(public_api)\\n29:     return public_api\\n30: \\n31: \\n32: add_docstring(\\n33:     _ArrayFunctionDispatcher,\\n34:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n35:     Class to wrap functions with checks for __array_function__ overrides.\\n36: \\n37:     All arguments are required, and can only be passed by position.\\n38: \\n39:     Parameters\\n40:     ----------\\n41:     dispatcher : function or None\\n42:         The dispatcher function that returns a single sequence-like object\\n43:         of all arguments relevant.  It must have the same signature (except\\n44:         the default values) as the actual implementation.\\n45:         If ``None``, this is a ``like=`` dispatcher and the\\n46:         ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher`` must be called with ``like`` as the\\n47:         first (additional and positional) argument.\\n48:     implementation : function\\n49:         Function that implements the operation on NumPy arrays without\\n50:         overrides.  Arguments passed calling the ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher``\\n51:         will be forwarded to this (and the ``dispatcher``) as if using\\n52:         ``*args, **kwargs``.\\n53: \\n54:     Attributes\\n55:     ----------\\n56:     _implementation : function\\n57:         The original implementation passed in.\\n58:     \\\"\\\"\\\")\\n59: \\n60: \\n61: # exposed for testing purposes; used internally by _ArrayFunctionDispatcher\\n62: add_docstring(\\n63:     _get_implementing_args,\\n64:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n65:     Collect arguments on which to call __array_function__.\\n66: \\n67:     Parameters\\n68:     ----------\\n69:     relevant_args : iterable of array-like\\n70:         Iterable of possibly array-like arguments to check for\\n71:         __array_function__ methods.\\n72: \\n73:     Returns\\n74:     -------\\n75:     Sequence of arguments with __array_function__ methods, in the order in\\n76:     which they should be called.\\n77:     \\\"\\\"\\\")\\n78: \\n79: \\n80: ArgSpec = collections.namedtuple('ArgSpec', 'args varargs keywords defaults')\\n81: \\n82: \\n83: def verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher):\\n84:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Verify that a dispatcher function has the right signature.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n85:     implementation_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(implementation))\\n86:     dispatcher_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(dispatcher))\\n87: \\n88:     if (implementation_spec.args != dispatcher_spec.args or\\n89:             implementation_spec.varargs != dispatcher_spec.varargs or\\n90:             implementation_spec.keywords != dispatcher_spec.keywords or\\n91:             (bool(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\\n92:              bool(dispatcher_spec.defaults)) or\\n93:             (implementation_spec.defaults is not None and\\n94:              len(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\\n95:              len(dispatcher_spec.defaults))):\\n96:         raise RuntimeError('implementation and dispatcher for %s have '\\n97:                            'different function signatures' % implementation)\\n98: \\n99:     if implementation_spec.defaults is not None:\\n100:         if dispatcher_spec.defaults != (None,) * len(dispatcher_spec.defaults):\\n101:             raise RuntimeError('dispatcher functions can only use None for '\\n102:                                'default argument values')\\n103: \\n104: \\n105: def array_function_dispatch(dispatcher=None, module=None, verify=True,\\n106:                             docs_from_dispatcher=False):\\n107:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Decorator for adding dispatch with the __array_function__ protocol.\\n108: \\n109:     See NEP-18 for example usage.\\n110: \\n111:     Parameters\\n112:     ----------\\n113:     dispatcher : callable or None\\n114:         Function that when called like ``dispatcher(*args, **kwargs)`` with\\n115:         arguments from the NumPy function call returns an iterable of\\n116:         array-like arguments to check for ``__array_function__``.\\n117: \\n118:         If `None`, the first argument is used as the single `like=` argument\\n119:         and not passed on.  A function implementing `like=` must call its\\n120:         dispatcher with `like` as the first non-keyword argument.\\n121:     module : str, optional\\n122:         __module__ attribute to set on new function, e.g., ``module='numpy'``.\\n123:         By default, module is copied from the decorated function.\\n124:     verify : bool, optional\\n125:         If True, verify the that the signature of the dispatcher and decorated\\n126:         function signatures match exactly: all required and optional arguments\\n127:         should appear in order with the same names, but the default values for\\n128:         all optional arguments should be ``None``. Only disable verification\\n129:         if the dispatcher's signature needs to deviate for some particular\\n130:         reason, e.g., because the function has a signature like\\n131:         ``func(*args, **kwargs)``.\\n132:     docs_from_dispatcher : bool, optional\\n133:         If True, copy docs from the dispatcher function onto the dispatched\\n134:         function, rather than from the implementation. This is useful for\\n135:         functions defined in C, which otherwise don't have docstrings.\\n136: \\n137:     Returns\\n138:     -------\\n139:     Function suitable for decorating the implementation of a NumPy function.\\n140: \\n141:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n142:     def decorator(implementation):\\n143:         if verify:\\n144:             if dispatcher is not None:\\n145:                 verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher)\\n146:             else:\\n147:                 # Using __code__ directly similar to verify_matching_signature\\n148:                 co = implementation.__code__\\n149:                 last_arg = co.co_argcount + co.co_kwonlyargcount - 1\\n150:                 last_arg = co.co_varnames[last_arg]\\n151:                 if last_arg != \\\"like\\\" or co.co_kwonlyargcount == 0:\\n152:                     raise RuntimeError(\\n153:                         \\\"__array_function__ expects `like=` to be the last \\\"\\n154:                         \\\"argument and a keyword-only argument. \\\"\\n155:                         f\\\"{implementation} does not seem to comply.\\\")\\n156: \\n157:         if docs_from_dispatcher:\\n158:             add_docstring(implementation, dispatcher.__doc__)\\n159: \\n160:         public_api = _ArrayFunctionDispatcher(dispatcher, implementation)\\n161:         public_api = functools.wraps(implementation)(public_api)\\n162: \\n163:         if module is not None:\\n164:             public_api.__module__ = module\\n165: \\n166:         ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\\n167: \\n168:         return public_api\\n169: \\n170:     return decorator\\n171: \\n172: \\n173: def array_function_from_dispatcher(\\n174:         implementation, module=None, verify=True, docs_from_dispatcher=True):\\n175:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Like array_function_dispatcher, but with function arguments flipped.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n176: \\n177:     def decorator(dispatcher):\\n178:         return array_function_dispatch(\\n179:             dispatcher, module, verify=verify,\\n180:             docs_from_dispatcher=docs_from_dispatcher)(implementation)\\n181:     return decorator\\n182: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/numpy--v2.2.2@9ca88bff35bf60de74f9f95e6f90e6b4e0913e78/-/blob/numpy/_core/overrides.py\"}",

    "content": "1: \"\"\"Implementation of __array_function__ overrides from NEP-18.\"\"\"\n2: import collections\n3: import functools\n4: \n5: from .._utils import set_module\n6: from .._utils._inspect import getargspec\n7: from numpy._core._multiarray_umath import (\n8:     add_docstring,  _get_implementing_args, _ArrayFunctionDispatcher)\n9: \n10: \n11: ARRAY_FUNCTIONS = set()\n12: \n13: array_function_like_doc = (\n14:     \"\"\"like : array_like, optional\n15:         Reference object to allow the creation of arrays which are not\n16:         NumPy arrays. If an array-like passed in as ``like`` supports\n17:         the ``__array_function__`` protocol, the result will be defined\n18:         by it. In this case, it ensures the creation of an array object\n19:         compatible with that passed in via this argument.\"\"\"\n20: )\n21: \n22: def get_array_function_like_doc(public_api, docstring_template=\"\"):\n23:     ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\n24:     docstring = public_api.__doc__ or docstring_template\n25:     return docstring.replace(\"${ARRAY_FUNCTION_LIKE}\", array_function_like_doc)\n26: \n27: def finalize_array_function_like(public_api):\n28:     public_api.__doc__ = get_array_function_like_doc(public_api)\n29:     return public_api\n30: \n31: \n32: add_docstring(\n33:     _ArrayFunctionDispatcher,\n34:     \"\"\"\n35:     Class to wrap functions with checks for __array_function__ overrides.\n36: \n37:     All arguments are required, and can only be passed by position.\n38: \n39:     Parameters\n40:     ----------\n41:     dispatcher : function or None\n42:         The dispatcher function that returns a single sequence-like object\n43:         of all arguments relevant.  It must have the same signature (except\n44:         the default values) as the actual implementation.\n45:         If ``None``, this is a ``like=`` dispatcher and the\n46:         ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher`` must be called with ``like`` as the\n47:         first (additional and positional) argument.\n48:     implementation : function\n49:         Function that implements the operation on NumPy arrays without\n50:         overrides.  Arguments passed calling the ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher``\n51:         will be forwarded to this (and the ``dispatcher``) as if using\n52:         ``*args, **kwargs``.\n53: \n54:     Attributes\n55: _ArrayFunctionDispatcher\n62: add_docstring(\n63:     _get_implementing_args,\n64:     \"\"\"\n65:     Collect arguments on which to call __array_function__.\n66: \n67:     Parameters\n68: -------\n75:     Sequence of arguments with __array_function__ methods, in the order in\n76:     which they should be called.\n77:     \"\"\")\n78: \n79: \n80: ArgSpec = collections.namedtuple('ArgSpec', 'args varargs keywords defaults')\n81: \n82: \n83: def verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher):\n84:     \"\"\"Verify that a dispatcher function has the right signature.\"\"\"\n85:     implementation_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(implementation))\n86:     dispatcher_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(dispatcher))\n87: \n88:     if (implementation_spec.args != dispatcher_spec.args or\n89:             implementation_spec.varargs != dispatcher_spec.varargs or\n90:             implementation_spec.keywords != dispatcher_spec.keywords or\n91:             (bool(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\n92:              bool(dispatcher_spec.defaults)) or\n93:             (implementation_spec.defaults is not None and\n94:              len(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\n95:              len(dispatcher_spec.defaults))):\n96:         raise RuntimeError('implementation and dispatcher for %s have '\n97:                            'different function signatures' % implementation)\n98: \n99:     if implementation_spec.defaults is not None:\n100:         if dispatcher_spec.defaults != (None,) * len(dispatcher_spec.defaults):\n101:             raise RuntimeError('dispatcher functions can only use None for '\n102:                                'default argument values')\n103: \n104: \n105: def array_function_dispatch(dispatcher=None, module=None, verify=True,\n106:                             docs_from_dispatcher=False):\n107:     \"\"\"Decorator for adding dispatch with the __array_function__ protocol.\n108: \n109:     See NEP-18 for example usage.\n110: \n111:     Parameters\n112:     ----------\n113:     dispatcher : callable or None\n114:         Function that when called like ``dispatcher(*args, **kwargs)`` with\n115:         arguments from the NumPy function call returns an iterable of\n116:         array-like arguments to check for ``__array_function__``.\n117: \n118:         If `None`, the first argument is used as the single `like=` argument\n119:         and not passed on.  A function implementing `like=` must call its\n120:         dispatcher with `like` as the first non-keyword argument.\n121:     module : str, optional\n122:         __module__ attribute to set on new function, e.g., ``module='numpy'``.\n123:         By default, module is copied from the decorated function.\n124:     verify : bool, optional\n125:         If True, verify the that the signature of the dispatcher and decorated\n126:         function signatures match exactly: all required and optional arguments\n127:         should appear in order with the same names, but the default values for\n128:         all optional arguments should be ``None``. Only disable verification\n129:         if the dispatcher's signature needs to deviate for some particular\n130:         reason, e.g., because the function has a signature like\n131:         ``func(*args, **kwargs)``.\n132:     docs_from_dispatcher : bool, optional\n133:         If True, copy docs from the dispatcher function onto the dispatched\n134:         function, rather than from the implementation. This is useful for\n135:         functions defined in C, which otherwise don't have docstrings.\n136: \n137:     Returns\n138:     -------\n139:     Function suitable for decorating the implementation of a NumPy function.\n140: \n141:     \"\"\"\n142:     def decorator(implementation):\n143:         if verify:\n144:             if dispatcher is not None:\n145:                 verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher)\n146:             else:\n147:                 # Using __code__ directly similar to verify_matching_signature\n148:                 co = implementation.__code__\n149:                 last_arg = co.co_argcount + co.co_kwonlyargcount - 1\n150:                 last_arg = co.co_varnames[last_arg]\n151:                 if last_arg != \"like\" or co.co_kwonlyargcount == 0:\n152:                     raise RuntimeError(\n153:                         \"__array_function__ expects `like=` to be the last \"\n154:                         \"argument and a keyword-only argument. \"\n155:                         f\"{implementation} does not seem to comply.\")\n156: \n157:         if docs_from_dispatcher:\n158:             add_docstring(implementation, dispatcher.__doc__)\n159: \n160:         public_api = _ArrayFunctionDispatcher(dispatcher, implementation)\n161:         public_api = functools.wraps(implementation)(public_api)\n162: \n163:         if module is not None:\n164:             public_api.__module__ = module\n165: \n166:         ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\n167: \n168:         return public_api\n169: \n170:     return decorator\n171: \n172: \n173: def array_function_from_dispatcher(\n174:         implementation, module=None, verify=True, docs_from_dispatcher=True):\n175:     \"\"\"Like array_function_dispatcher, but with function arguments flipped.\"\"\"\n176: \n177:     def decorator(dispatcher):\n178:         return array_function_dispatch(\n179:             dispatcher, module, verify=verify,\n180:             docs_from_dispatcher=docs_from_dispatcher)(implementation)\n181:     return decorator\n182: ",

Input
{
  "content": "\"\"\"Implementation of __array_function__ overrides from NEP-18.\"\"\"\nimport collections\nimport functools\n\nfrom .._utils import set_module\nfrom .._utils._inspect import getargspec\nfrom numpy._core._multiarray_umath import (\n    add_docstring,  _get_implementing_args, _ArrayFunctionDispatcher)\n\n\nARRAY_FUNCTIONS = set()\n\narray_function_like_doc = (\n    \"\"\"like : array_like, optional\n        Reference object to allow the creation of arrays which are not\n        NumPy arrays. If an array-like passed in as ``like`` supports\n        the ``__array_function__`` protocol, the result will be defined\n        by it. In this case, it ensures the creation of an array object\n        compatible with that passed in via this argument.\"\"\"\n)\n\ndef get_array_function_like_doc(public_api, docstring_template=\"\"):\n    ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\n    docstring = public_api.__doc__ or docstring_template\n    return docstring.replace(\"${ARRAY_FUNCTION_LIKE}\", array_function_like_doc)\n\ndef finalize_array_function_like(public_api):\n    public_api.__doc__ = get_array_function_like_doc(public_api)\n    return public_api\n\n\nadd_docstring(\n    _ArrayFunctionDispatcher,\n    \"\"\"\n    Class to wrap functions with checks for __array_function__ overrides.\n\n    All arguments are required, and can only be passed by position.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    dispatcher : function or None\n        The dispatcher function that returns a single sequence-like object\n        of all arguments relevant.  It must have the same signature (except\n        the default values) as the actual implementation.\n        If ``None``, this is a ``like=`` dispatcher and the\n        ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher`` must be called with ``like`` as the\n        first (additional and positional) argument.\n    implementation : function\n        Function that implements the operation on NumPy arrays without\n        overrides.  Arguments passed calling the ``_ArrayFunctionDispatcher``\n        will be forwarded to this (and the ``dispatcher``) as if using\n        ``*args, **kwargs``.\n\n    Attributes\n    ----------\n    _implementation : function\n        The original implementation passed in.\n    \"\"\")\n\n\n# exposed for testing purposes; used internally by _ArrayFunctionDispatcher\nadd_docstring(\n    _get_implementing_args,\n    \"\"\"\n    Collect arguments on which to call __array_function__.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    relevant_args : iterable of array-like\n        Iterable of possibly array-like arguments to check for\n        __array_function__ methods.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Sequence of arguments with __array_function__ methods, in the order in\n    which they should be called.\n    \"\"\")\n\n\nArgSpec = collections.namedtuple('ArgSpec', 'args varargs keywords defaults')\n\n\ndef verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher):\n    \"\"\"Verify that a dispatcher function has the right signature.\"\"\"\n    implementation_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(implementation))\n    dispatcher_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(dispatcher))\n\n    if (implementation_spec.args != dispatcher_spec.args or\n            implementation_spec.varargs != dispatcher_spec.varargs or\n            implementation_spec.keywords != dispatcher_spec.keywords or\n            (bool(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\n             bool(dispatcher_spec.defaults)) or\n            (implementation_spec.defaults is not None and\n             len(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\n             len(dispatcher_spec.defaults))):\n        raise RuntimeError('implementation and dispatcher for %s have '\n                           'different function signatures' % implementation)\n\n    if implementation_spec.defaults is not None:\n        if dispatcher_spec.defaults != (None,) * len(dispatcher_spec.defaults):\n            raise RuntimeError('dispatcher functions can only use None for '\n                               'default argument values')\n\n\ndef dispatch_array_function(dispatcher=None, module=None, verify=True,\n                            docs_from_dispatcher=False):\n    \"\"\"Decorator for adding dispatch with the __array_function__ protocol.\n\n    See NEP-18 for example usage.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    dispatcher : callable or None\n        Function that when called like ``dispatcher(*args, **kwargs)`` with\n        arguments from the NumPy function call returns an iterable of\n        array-like arguments to check for ``__array_function__``.\n\n        If `None`, the first argument is used as the single `like=` argument\n        and not passed on.  A function implementing `like=` must call its\n        dispatcher with `like` as the first non-keyword argument.\n    module : str, optional\n        __module__ attribute to set on new function, e.g., ``module='numpy'``.\n        By default, module is copied from the decorated function.\n    verify : bool, optional\n        If True, verify the that the signature of the dispatcher and decorated\n        function signatures match exactly: all required and optional arguments\n        should appear in order with the same names, but the default values for\n        all optional arguments should be ``None``. Only disable verification\n        if the dispatcher's signature needs to deviate for some particular\n        reason, e.g., because the function has a signature like\n        ``func(*args, **kwargs)``.\n    docs_from_dispatcher : bool, optional\n        If True, copy docs from the dispatcher function onto the dispatched\n        function, rather than from the implementation. This is useful for\n        functions defined in C, which otherwise don't have docstrings.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Function suitable for decorating the implementation of a NumPy function.\n\n    \"\"\"\n    def decorator(implementation):\n        if verify:\n            if dispatcher is not None:\n                verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher)\n            else:\n                # Using __code__ directly similar to verify_matching_signature\n                co = implementation.__code__\n                last_arg = co.co_argcount + co.co_kwonlyargcount - 1\n                last_arg = co.co_varnames[last_arg]\n                if last_arg != \"like\" or co.co_kwonlyargcount == 0:\n                    raise RuntimeError(\n                        \"__array_function__ expects `like=` to be the last \"\n                        \"argument and a keyword-only argument. \"\n                        f\"{implementation} does not seem to comply.\")\n\n        if docs_from_dispatcher:\n            add_docstring(implementation, dispatcher.__doc__)\n\n        public_api = _ArrayFunctionDispatcher(dispatcher, implementation)\n        public_api = functools.wraps(implementation)(public_api)\n\n        if module is not None:\n            public_api.__module__ = module\n\n        ARRAY_FUNCTIONS.add(public_api)\n\n        return public_api\n\n    return decorator\n\n\ndef array_function_from_dispatcher(\n        implementation, module=None, verify=True, docs_from_dispatcher=True):\n    \"\"\"Like array_function_dispatcher, but with function arguments flipped.\"\"\"\n\n    def decorator(dispatcher):\n        return dispatch_array_function(\n            dispatcher, module, verify=verify,\n            docs_from_dispatcher=docs_from_dispatcher)(implementation)\n    return decorator\n\n\n# Backward compatibility alias\narray_function_dispatch = dispatch_array_function\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/numpy/_core/overrides.py"
}

possibly array-like arguments to check for\n        __array_function__ methods.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    Sequence of arguments with __array_function__ methods, in the order in\n    which they should be called.\n    \"\"\")\n\n\nArgSpec = collections.namedtuple('ArgSpec', 'args varargs keywords defaults')\n\n\ndef verify_matching_signatures(implementation, dispatcher):\n    \"\"\"Verify that a dispatcher function has the right signature.\"\"\"\n    implementation_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(implementation))\n    dispatcher_spec = ArgSpec(*getargspec(dispatcher))\n\n    if (implementation_spec.args != dispatcher_spec.args or\n            implementation_spec.varargs != dispatcher_spec.varargs or\n            implementation_spec.keywords != dispatcher_spec.keywords or\n            (bool(implementation_spec.defaults) !=\n             bool(dispatcher_spec.defaults)) or\n            (implementation_spec.defaults is not None and\n function signatures' % implementation)\n\n    if implementation_spec.defaults is not None:\n        if dispatcher_spec.defaults != (None,) * len(dispatcher_spec.defaults):\n            raise RuntimeError('dispatcher functions can only use None for '\n                               'default argument values')\n\n\ndef dispatch_array_function(dispatcher=None, module=None, verify=True,\n                            docs_from_dispatcher=False):\n    \"\"\"Decorator for adding dispatch with the __array_function__ protocol.\n\n    See NEP-18 for example usage.\n\n    Parameters\n
